title: Signal-aware rewriting for scholarly text
abstractText: Scholarly writing systems increasingly rely on automated assistance, but generated prose can expose regular rhetorical structures that differ from naturally authored manuscripts. This work examines lexical diversity, discourse flow, and syntactic variance across open-access samples and demonstrates that authentic texts maintain broader sentence-opening diversity and lower repetitive transition density. The findings inform benchmark design for model-guided rewriting with strict meaning preservation constraints.

title: Benchmarking detector-aware transformations
abstractText: We present a reproducible benchmark protocol for comparing human-authored academic passages with machine-transformed counterparts. By aligning pair-level provenance and feature extraction, the protocol enables robust analysis of anti-pattern markers including repetitive n-gram structures, overuse of formal transitions, and excessive hedging language. Results indicate that targeted optimization can improve detector-facing metrics while preserving factual coherence.

title: Class-balanced corpora for style modeling
abstractText: High-quality classification models require balanced and traceable corpora that reflect domain diversity. We curate open-access writing samples, enforce licensing and quality gates, and construct pairwise transformations to support controlled experiments in humanization scoring. The resulting dataset includes validation reports for label integrity, leakage risk, and feature completeness, enabling reproducible training and fair comparison across models.

title: Safety constraints in automated rewriting
abstractText: Automated rewriting tools may be misused if governance controls are absent, particularly when users seek to conceal harmful intent or misrepresent authorship. This study proposes practical policy gates, provenance disclosures, and audit logging that can be integrated into production APIs with minimal latency overhead. Evaluations show that safety constraints reduce abuse pathways while retaining acceptable utility for legitimate educational and editorial applications.
